name: Altruist
ai_strategy_summary: Altruist is the most disruptive AI-native custodian in wealth management. Its AI platform Hazel — launched September 2025, built on the 2025 acquisition of AI startup Thyme — signed 1,600 RIA firms in its first month alone, with a pipeline suggesting 1,500 new advisors per month for the next nine months. The February 2026 launch of Hazel's tax planning capability coincided with a $130 billion sell-off across Schwab, LPL, Raymond James, and Ameriprise stocks (CNBC, Feb 2026). Hazel's architectural moat is custodial integration: it reads live from Altruist's custodial system of record (holdings, balances, cost basis, RMDs, beneficiaries), enabling advisors to query real-time account data in AI conversations. The $152M Series F (led by GIC, April 2025) valued Altruist at $1.9B. CEO Jason Wenk frames the custodian as 'the ultimate system of record' for advisor AI.
headline_metric: 1,600 RIA firms in month 1; $130B stock sell-off (CNBC, Feb 2026); $1.9B valuation; 1,000+ wealth managers on Hazel
headline_initiative: Hazel: AI-native custodial platform with live account data integration + tax planning

advisor_productivity.headline: Hazel: 1,600 RIA firms in 30 days, live custodial data in every AI conversation, 1,000+ wealth managers
advisor_productivity.detail: Hazel unifies CRM, email, meeting notes, and live custodial data (holdings, balances, cost basis, RMDs, beneficiaries) in a single AI conversation layer — the first platform to do so. Advisors can run household-level concentration analysis in seconds, identify clients behind on RMDs, harvest unrealised losses, and generate personalised tax strategies from 1040s and pay stubs. Over 1,000 wealth managers adopted Hazel within months of its September 2025 launch. The February 2026 addition of AI tax planning coincided with $130B in market value loss across legacy custodians (CNBC, Feb 2026).
evidence: 1,600 RIA firms subscribed in first month, pipeline of 1,500/month for 9 months (RIABiz, March 2026)
1,000+ wealth managers using Hazel since September 2025 launch (Business Wire, Nov 2025)
$130B sell-off across Schwab, LPL, Raymond James, Ameriprise (CNBC, Feb 2026)
First custodian with live account data in AI conversation layer (Business Wire)
Household-level analysis, RMD tracking, tax-loss harvesting in seconds (Altruist Newsroom)
Gerber Kawasaki ($4B AUM) on platform (Business Wire)

client_personalization.headline: AI-generated personalised tax strategies from 1040s and pay stubs in minutes
client_personalization.detail: Hazel reads and interprets 1040 tax returns, pay stubs, and account statements to generate fully personalised tax strategies. The AI creates interactive graphs for analysis and incorporates meeting prep, email drafting, and firm-wide reporting. This capability directly addresses the cross-sell visibility gap that Datos Insights identified as the key opportunity for advisor AI.
evidence: Tax planning from 1040s, pay stubs, account statements (Business Wire, Feb 2026)
Interactive graphs for richer analysis (Altruist product documentation)
Datos Insights analysis: cross-sell gap is the key AI opportunity (our intelligence entry)

new_business_models.headline: AI-native custodian model at $600-$1,500/seat disrupting legacy custodians valued on AUM-fee economics
new_business_models.detail: Altruist's pricing model ($600-$1,500 per seat for full custodial + AI capabilities) structurally undercuts legacy custodians. The $130B stock sell-off proved the market has priced in the thesis that AI-native platforms can disintermediate legacy custodians. The architectural advantage — AI reading directly from the custodial system of record — cannot be replicated by partnerships or bolt-on integrations. Nick Beim (Venrock partner) described this as the competitive moat: 'Hazel is different because it sits inside the custodial system.'
evidence: $130B sell-off across legacy custodian stocks (CNBC, Invezz, Feb 2026)
Nick Beim thesis on custodial moat (our TL entry: beim-week-altruist-shook-markets)
Sowell Management enterprise agreement adding Altruist as custodian (InvestmentNews, 2026)
